vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1723

narratives.Description: Port the WIP-cap re-validation gate (scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py) into a pure evaluate() in @deftai/core plus a thin CLI, preserving the three-state exit (0 within cap / 1 over cap / 2 config error), the --allow-over-cap escape hatch, the relief-hint refusal text, and the within-cap success banner. Reuses the already-landed @deftai/core/policy WIP helpers (resolveWipCap, countVbriefWip) and proves parity against the Python oracle via cache-off golden-diff fixtures.
narratives.Overview:
## Parent

#1530 (Part 1 of 2; umbrella epic #1545)

## What to build

Port the WIP-cap gate to TS, preserving the `plan.policy.wipCap` enforcement, the relief hint, and `--allow-over-cap` semantics.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `verify:wip-cap` runs on TS with the cap enforcement preserved
- [ ] Golden-output parity vs the Python oracle (cache-off)
- [ ] `task check` passes

## Standing invariants (all Part-1 children)
- The Python suite is the **parity oracle** and runs **wholesale** (not affected-sliced) until deleted.
- `task check` stays green at every step (strangler-fig; no big-bang).
- The parity gate runs **cache-off** so a golden diff is never served from cache.
- No Python module is deleted until its TS replacement covers historical bug fixtures (not just happy-path) and CI usage.
- No agent-runtime dependency (that is Part 2, #1707); no monorepo orchestrator on day one (Nx never; Turborepo only on a measured trigger).

## Type

AFK

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #1718
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add packages/core/src/wip-cap/ with evaluate.ts (pure (projectRoot, {allowOverCap}) -> {code, message}) and index.ts. Reuse the already-landed @deftai/core/policy WIP helpers (resolveWipCap, countVbriefWip, DEFAULT_WIP_CAP) so cap resolution and the pending/+active/ count share a single source of truth.
- Preserve all branches of scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py: within-cap success banner (exit 0), over-cap refusal with the canonical relief verbs (exit 1), --allow-over-cap framework grace (exit 0 with the OVER-cap warning), and the malformed-PROJECT-DEFINITION config error (exit 2). Match the --quiet behavior.
- Add a thin packages/cli/src/verify-wip-cap.ts CLI plus a wip-cap-parity.ts harness that golden-diffs the TS output against scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py with cache off across fixtures covering every exit state. Cover the evaluate branches with vitest fixtures.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer, I want the WIP-cap gate ported to TypeScript, so that cap re-validation no longer depends on the Python runtime.

items.title: verify:wip-cap on TypeScript preserves the three-state exit

narrative.Acceptance: verify:wip-cap on TypeScript returns exit 0 within cap, returns exit 1 when it rejects an over-cap state, and returns exit 2 on a malformed PROJECT-DEFINITION, including the --allow-over-cap exit-0 grace path, matching the Python oracle.

items.title: Golden-output parity vs the Python oracle (cache-off)

narrative.Acceptance: wip-cap-parity renders byte-identical banner and refusal text and returns the same exit code as scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py across fixtures covering all three exit states, with cache off.

references.title: Issue #1723: feat(ts-migration): port verify:wip-cap to TS (Wave 2)

expected_outputs: wip-cap-parity exits 0 with byte-identical three-state output versus the Python oracle scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py (cache off)
vitest coverage >=85% across packages/core/src/wip-cap and packages/cli/src/verify-wip-cap.ts
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to the Python oracle scripts/preflight_wip_cap.py via cache-off golden-diff parity fixtures, not a spec section.
